tasks.title: Initialize workspace crate structure and protocol library foundation
tasks.description: Create the complete crate directory structure and implement core protocol types, error handling, and shared data structures in eoip-proto
tasks.details:
Create directories: crates/{eoip-proto,eoip-api,eoip-helper,eoip-rs}/src. Write Cargo.toml for eoip-proto with deps: bytes="1", thiserror="2". Implement:
- src/types.rs: DemuxKey(tunnel_id: u16, peer_addr: IpAddr), TunnelId newtype, TunnelConfig/TunnelStats structs with AtomicU64 fields (tx/rx packets/bytes/errors, last_rx/tx timestamps)
- src/error.rs: EoipError enum with thiserror derives covering InvalidGreHeader, InvalidEtherIpHeader, UnknownTunnel, PacketTooShort, TapError, RawSocket, HelperDisconnected, Config errors
- src/wire.rs: Helper<->Daemon protocol messages (HelperMsg: TapCreated/RawSocket/Error/HelperReady, DaemonMsg: CreateTunnel/DestroyTunnel/Shutdown) with serde serialization
- src/lib.rs: Re-export all public types

Use #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)] for DemuxKey. Document all public APIs with /// comments explaining wire format compatibility.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests verifying: DemuxKey equality/hashing behavior, error type Display implementations match expected messages, wire protocol message serialization roundtrips correctly

subtasks.title: Create workspace directory structure and eoip-proto crate skeleton
subtasks.description: Create all required crate directories (crates/{eoip-proto,eoip-api,eoip-helper,eoip-rs}/src) and initialize the eoip-proto crate with its Cargo.toml configuration and lib.rs entry point
subtasks.details: Execute: mkdir -p crates/{eoip-proto,eoip-api,eoip-helper,eoip-rs}/src. Create crates/eoip-proto/Cargo.toml with package metadata (name="eoip-proto", version.workspace=true, edition.workspace=true), dependencies (bytes.workspace=true, thiserror.workspace=true), and documentation fields. Create crates/eoip-proto/src/lib.rs with module declarations (pub mod types; pub mod error; pub mod gre; pub mod etherip; pub mod udp_shim; pub mod wire;) and top-level doc comments explaining the protocol library purpose. Verify workspace membership is already configured in root Cargo.toml.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify with 'cargo check -p eoip-proto' that crate compiles (will initially fail due to missing modules). Confirm directory structure with 'ls -R crates/' showing all four crate directories with src/ subdirectories.

subtasks.title: Implement core types module with DemuxKey, TunnelId, and statistics structures
subtasks.description: Create src/types.rs implementing DemuxKey (tunnel_id + peer_addr tuple), TunnelId newtype wrapper, TunnelConfig, and TunnelStats with AtomicU64 counters for packet/byte/error tracking
subtasks.details: In crates/eoip-proto/src/types.rs: Define DemuxKey struct with #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)] containing tunnel_id: u16 and peer_addr: std::net::IpAddr. Create TunnelId newtype: pub struct TunnelId(pub u16); with validation method ensuring <= 4095 for EoIPv6 compatibility. Implement TunnelConfig struct with fields: tunnel_id: TunnelId, local_addr: IpAddr, remote_addr: IpAddr, mtu: u16, keepalive_interval: Duration, keepalive_timeout: Duration. Implement TunnelStats with atomic fields: tx_packets, tx_bytes, rx_packets, rx_bytes, tx_errors, rx_errors (all AtomicU64), plus last_rx_timestamp and last_tx_timestamp (AtomicI64 for Unix millis). Add /// doc comments on all public types explaining wire format constraints (e.g., TunnelId range limits for EoIPv6).
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: (1) DemuxKey hash collision test with 1000 random IDs, (2) TunnelId validation rejects values > 4095, (3) TunnelStats atomic operations from multiple threads verify no data races, (4) Ensure IpAddr enum works with both IPv4/IPv6 in DemuxKey equality checks.

subtasks.title: Implement error types module with comprehensive EoipError enum
subtasks.description: Create src/error.rs defining EoipError enum with thiserror derives covering all protocol validation failures, I/O errors, and configuration errors as specified in architecture docs
subtasks.details: In crates/eoip-proto/src/error.rs: Define #[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] pub enum EoipError with variants: InvalidGreHeader(String) with #[error("invalid GRE header: {0}")], InvalidEtherIpHeader(String) with #[error("invalid EtherIP header: {0}")], UnknownTunnel{tunnel_id: u16, peer_addr: IpAddr} with formatted error, PacketTooShort{got: usize, need: usize}, TunnelIdOutOfRange{id: u16, max: u16}, PayloadTooLarge{size: usize, limit: usize}, TapError{iface: String, source: std::io::Error}, RawSocketError(#[from] std::io::Error), HelperDisconnected, ConfigError(String), InvalidMagicBytes{expected: Vec<u8>, got: Vec<u8>}, InvalidVersion{expected: u8, got: u8}. Implement custom Display messages that include context about what was expected vs. received for debugging.
subtasks.testStrategy: Tests: (1) Verify Display implementations produce expected messages (e.g., PacketTooShort shows "got 7, need 8"), (2) Test std::io::Error conversion via #[from], (3) Verify all error variants implement Error trait and can be chained with .source(), (4) Test error serialization if implementing serde::Serialize for wire protocol.

subtasks.title: Implement helper-daemon wire protocol in src/wire.rs
subtasks.description: Create src/wire.rs implementing HelperMsg and DaemonMsg enums for IPC communication between privileged helper and unprivileged daemon, with serde serialization support
subtasks.details: In crates/eoip-proto/src/wire.rs: Add serde dependency to Cargo.toml. Define #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)] pub enum HelperMsg { TapCreated{iface_name: String, tunnel_id: u16}, RawSocket{address_family: u16 /* AF_INET=2 or AF_INET6=10 */}, Error{msg: String}, HelperReady }. Define matching DaemonMsg enum with variants: CreateTunnel{iface_name: String, tunnel_id: u16}, DestroyTunnel{iface_name: String}, Shutdown. Add helper functions: pub fn serialize_msg<T: Serialize>(msg: &T) -> Result<Vec<u8>, EoipError> using bincode/postcard (choose one) for compact binary serialization, and pub fn deserialize_msg<T: DeserializeOwned>(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<T, EoipError>. Document that FDs are passed separately via SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data (not in serialized payload).
subtasks.testStrategy: Tests: (1) Roundtrip serialization for all HelperMsg variants, (2) Roundtrip for all DaemonMsg variants, (3) Verify serialized size is compact (< 100 bytes for typical messages), (4) Test handling of malformed/truncated input returns appropriate EoipError, (5) Verify backward compatibility if enum variants are added (if using tagged format).

subtasks.title: Complete eoip-proto with lib.rs re-exports and documentation
subtasks.description: Finalize crates/eoip-proto/src/lib.rs by re-exporting all public types from submodules and adding comprehensive crate-level documentation with usage examples
subtasks.details: In crates/eoip-proto/src/lib.rs: Add #![doc = include_str!("../../docs/design/protocol.md")] to embed protocol specification in rustdoc. Create module declarations: pub mod types; pub mod error; pub mod wire; (gre, etherip, udp_shim will be added in later tasks). Re-export commonly used types at crate root: pub use types::{DemuxKey, TunnelId, TunnelConfig, TunnelStats}; pub use error::EoipError; pub use wire::{HelperMsg, DaemonMsg}; Add top-level doc comment: /// Pure Rust implementation of MikroTik EoIP/EoIPv6 protocol codecs. /// This crate provides zero-copy encode/decode for GRE-based EoIP (IPv4, protocol 47), /// RFC 3378 EtherIP (IPv6, protocol 97), and UDP encapsulation mode. Add /// # Examples section showing basic DemuxKey usage. Verify 'cargo doc --open -p eoip-proto' generates clean documentation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests in tests/lib_test.rs: (1) Import all public types without path qualification, (2) Verify DemuxKey can be created and hashed, (3) Verify EoipError variants are accessible, (4) Run 'cargo doc -p eoip-proto' and check for warnings, (5) Verify doctests compile and pass with 'cargo test --doc -p eoip-proto'.

tasks.title: Implement EoIP GRE header codec with mixed-endianness handling
tasks.description: Implement MikroTik-compatible EoIP header encode/decode in eoip-proto with byte-level precision for magic validation, big-endian payload length, and little-endian tunnel ID
tasks.details:
Create src/gre.rs with:
- encode_eoip_header(tunnel_id: u16, payload_len: u16, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<(), EoipError>: Write [0x20,0x01,0x64,0x00], payload_len.to_be_bytes(), tunnel_id.to_le_bytes()
- decode_eoip_header(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16 tunnel_id, u16 payload_len, usize header_len), EoipError>: Validate magic == [0x20,0x01,0x64,0x00], extract BE payload_len from buf[4..6], LE tunnel_id from buf[6..8]
- validate_eoip_packet(buf: &[u8], ip_payload_len: usize) -> Result<(), EoipError>: Check len>=8, magic correct, payload_len <= ip_payload_len-8, payload_len >= 14

Use bytes::BufMut for zero-copy writing. Return PacketTooShort if buf.len() < 8. Document mixed endianness with CRITICAL comment.
tasks.testStrategy: Property tests with proptest: encode→decode roundtrips for all u16 tunnel_ids. Byte-level tests verifying TID=42 produces [0x2A,0x00] at offset 6, payload_len=64 produces [0x00,0x40] at offset 4. Negative tests: wrong magic, short buffers, payload_len exceeds bounds

subtasks.title: Create gre.rs module skeleton with constants and function signatures
subtasks.description: Create the src/gre.rs file in eoip-proto crate with magic byte constants, function signatures, and comprehensive documentation about MikroTik EoIP header format
subtasks.details: Create crates/eoip-proto/src/gre.rs with: (1) Module-level doc comment explaining MikroTik EoIP uses non-standard GRE variant with 8-byte header format [magic:4B][payload_len:2B BE][tunnel_id:2B LE]. (2) Define public constant: pub const EOIP_MAGIC: [u8; 4] = [0x20, 0x01, 0x64, 0x00]; (3) Define EOIP_HEADER_LEN: usize = 8; (4) Add CRITICAL comment documenting mixed-endianness: payload_len is big-endian (network byte order) but tunnel_id is little-endian (x86 byte order) per MikroTik implementation. (5) Add function signatures: pub fn encode_eoip_header(tunnel_id: u16, payload_len: u16, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<(), EoipError>; pub fn decode_eoip_header(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16, u16, usize), EoipError>; pub fn validate_eoip_packet(buf: &[u8], ip_payload_len: usize) -> Result<(), EoipError>. (6) Add use statements: use crate::error::EoipError; use bytes::BufMut;
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify module compiles with 'cargo check -p eoip-proto'. Confirm constants are correctly defined by writing a simple test that checks EOIP_MAGIC == [0x20, 0x01, 0x64, 0x00] and EOIP_HEADER_LEN == 8.

subtasks.title: Implement encode_eoip_header with buffer length validation and mixed-endian writes
subtasks.description: Implement the encode_eoip_header function that writes the 8-byte MikroTik EoIP header with proper byte ordering: magic bytes, big-endian payload length, and little-endian tunnel ID
subtasks.details: In encode_eoip_header function: (1) Check buf.len() >= 8, return Err(EoipError::PacketTooShort{got: buf.len(), need: 8}) if insufficient. (2) Write magic bytes at offset 0-3: buf[0..4].copy_from_slice(&EOIP_MAGIC). (3) Write payload_len in big-endian at offset 4-5: buf[4..6].copy_from_slice(&payload_len.to_be_bytes()). (4) Write tunnel_id in little-endian at offset 6-7: buf[6..8].copy_from_slice(&tunnel_id.to_le_bytes()). (5) Return Ok(()). (6) Add comprehensive doc comment with example showing TID=100, payload_len=60 produces [0x20,0x01,0x64,0x00,0x00,0x3C,0x64,0x00]. (7) Add inline comment before tunnel_id write: // CRITICAL: tunnel_id is little-endian per MikroTik spec, NOT network byte order. Avoid using bytes::BufMut initially for simplicity - direct slice writes are clearer for exact byte positioning.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: (1) encode_eoip_header(42, 64, &mut buf) verifies buf == [0x20,0x01,0x64,0x00,0x00,0x40,0x2A,0x00] byte-by-byte. (2) Test with TID=0x1234, payload=0x5678 produces correct BE/LE bytes at expected offsets. (3) Test with 7-byte buffer returns PacketTooShort error. (4) Test buffer exactly 8 bytes succeeds.

subtasks.title: Implement decode_eoip_header with magic validation and mixed-endian reads
subtasks.description: Implement decode_eoip_header to parse the 8-byte EoIP header, validate magic bytes, and extract big-endian payload length and little-endian tunnel ID
subtasks.details: In decode_eoip_header function: (1) Check buf.len() >= 8, return Err(EoipError::PacketTooShort{got: buf.len(), need: 8}). (2) Validate magic bytes: if &buf[0..4] != EOIP_MAGIC, return Err(EoipError::InvalidMagicBytes{expected: EOIP_MAGIC.to_vec(), got: buf[0..4].to_vec()}). (3) Extract payload_len in big-endian: let payload_len = u16::from_be_bytes([buf[4], buf[5]]). (4) Extract tunnel_id in little-endian: let tunnel_id = u16::from_le_bytes([buf[6], buf[7]]). (5) Return Ok((tunnel_id, payload_len, EOIP_HEADER_LEN)). (6) Add doc comment explaining return tuple: (tunnel_id, payload_len, header_length), where header_length is always 8 for standard EoIP. (7) Add inline comment: // CRITICAL: Read tunnel_id as little-endian, NOT big-endian.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: (1) Decode [0x20,0x01,0x64,0x00,0x00,0x40,0x2A,0x00] returns (42, 64, 8). (2) Worked example: [0x20,0x01,0x64,0x00,0x12,0x34,0xAB,0xCD] returns tunnel_id=0xCDAB (52651), payload_len=0x1234 (4660). (3) Wrong magic [0x20,0x01,0x64,0x01,...] returns InvalidMagicBytes error. (4) 7-byte buffer returns PacketTooShort. (5) Verify decode(encode(tid, len)) roundtrips correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement validate_eoip_packet with Ethernet frame size constraints
subtasks.description: Implement validate_eoip_packet to perform holistic packet validation including header presence, magic bytes, and payload length bounds checking against IP payload size and minimum Ethernet frame requirements
subtasks.details: In validate_eoip_packet function taking buf: &[u8] and ip_payload_len: usize: (1) Check buf.len() >= 8, return PacketTooShort error. (2) Validate magic bytes match EOIP_MAGIC, return InvalidGreHeader error with descriptive message if mismatch. (3) Extract payload_len via decode_eoip_header (reuse existing function) or inline read. (4) Validate payload_len >= 14 (minimum Ethernet frame: 6B dst + 6B src + 2B type), return Err(EoipError::PayloadTooSmall{got: payload_len, min: 14}) if smaller. (5) Validate payload_len <= ip_payload_len - 8 (must fit in IP payload after header), return Err(EoipError::PayloadTooLarge{size: payload_len as usize, limit: ip_payload_len - 8}) if exceeds. (6) Validate total buf.len() >= 8 + payload_len as usize, return PacketTooShort if incomplete. (7) Return Ok(()). Add doc comment explaining this validates packet is well-formed before demux lookup.
subtasks.testStrategy: Tests: (1) Valid packet: 8B header + 60B payload with ip_payload_len=68 passes. (2) payload_len=13 (too small for Ethernet) returns PayloadTooSmall error. (3) payload_len=100 but ip_payload_len=50 returns PayloadTooLarge. (4) Truncated buffer (buf.len()=10, payload_len=60) returns PacketTooShort. (5) Wrong magic bytes caught. (6) Boundary: payload_len=14 (minimum valid Ethernet) passes.

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive property tests and integration tests for GRE codec
subtasks.description: Create property-based tests using proptest to verify encode/decode roundtrips for all valid tunnel IDs and payload lengths, plus integration tests demonstrating real-world packet handling
subtasks.details: In crates/eoip-proto/src/gre.rs add #[cfg(test)] mod tests: (1) Add proptest dependency to Cargo.toml dev-dependencies. (2) Write proptest: proptest! { #[test] fn roundtrip_all_tunnel_ids(tid in 0u16..=65535, plen in 14u16..=1500) { let mut buf = vec![0u8; 8]; encode_eoip_header(tid, plen, &mut buf).unwrap(); let (decoded_tid, decoded_plen, hdr_len) = decode_eoip_header(&buf).unwrap(); assert_eq!(decoded_tid, tid); assert_eq!(decoded_plen, plen); assert_eq!(hdr_len, 8); } }. (3) Add byte-exact tests: test_encode_tid_42() verifying buf[6]==0x2A && buf[7]==0x00, test_encode_payload_len_64() verifying buf[4]==0x00 && buf[5]==0x40. (4) Add negative tests: test_short_buffer_encode() with 7-byte buf, test_short_buffer_decode() with 7-byte buf, test_wrong_magic() with corrupted magic bytes. (5) Add integration test in tests/gre_integration.rs: create full EoIP packet with Ethernet payload (14B header + 46B data), encode header, validate, decode, verify payload extraction.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run 'cargo test -p eoip-proto gre' to execute all tests. Verify proptest runs 256+ iterations. Confirm coverage of: (a) all u16 tunnel_id values via proptest, (b) byte-level endianness verification, (c) boundary conditions (payload_len=14, tid=0, tid=65535), (d) error paths (short buffers, invalid magic), (e) full encode→validate→decode workflow. Run with 'cargo test -- --nocapture' to see any debug output.

tasks.title: Implement EoIPv6/EtherIP header codec with 12-bit tunnel ID nibble-packing
tasks.description: Implement RFC 3378-based EoIPv6 header encode/decode with MikroTik tunnel ID encoding in reserved bits
tasks.details:
Create src/etherip.rs with:
- encode_eoipv6_header(tunnel_id: u16, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<(), EoipError>: Validate tid <= 4095, write buf[0] = ((tid>>8) as u8)<<4 | 0x03, buf[1] = (tid&0xFF) as u8
- decode_eoipv6_header(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16 tunnel_id, usize header_len), EoipError>: Check buf.len()>=2, verify (buf[0]&0x0F)==0x03, extract tid = ((buf[0] as u16 >> 4) << 8) | buf[1] as u16
- validate_eoipv6_packet(buf: &[u8], ipv6_payload_len: usize) -> Result<(), EoipError>: Check len>=16 (2B hdr+14B eth), version==0x3

Return error if tunnel_id > 4095 on encode. Document that tid=0 produces RFC 3378-compliant [0x03,0x00].
tasks.testStrategy: Worked example tests: TID=300 (0x12C) produces [0x13,0x2C], TID=1000 (0x3E8) produces [0x33,0xE8]. Property tests: encode→decode for TID 0-4095. Boundary tests: TID=4095 OK, TID=4096 returns error. Version validation rejects 0x02/0x04

subtasks.title: Create etherip.rs module with EoIPv6 constants and function signatures
subtasks.description: Create the src/etherip.rs file in eoip-proto crate with RFC 3378 version constant, header length definition, function signatures, and comprehensive documentation about MikroTik's 12-bit tunnel ID nibble-packing into reserved bits
subtasks.details: Create crates/eoip-proto/src/etherip.rs with: (1) Module-level doc comment explaining RFC 3378 EtherIP format with 2-byte header [version:4b=0x3][reserved:12b] and how MikroTik encodes tunnel_id in reserved bits as [tid_hi:4b][version:4b=0x3][tid_lo:8b]. (2) Define public constants: pub const ETHERIP_VERSION: u8 = 0x03; pub const ETHERIP_HEADER_LEN: usize = 2; pub const MAX_EOIPV6_TUNNEL_ID: u16 = 4095; (3) Add detailed comment explaining bit layout: Byte 0 bits[7:4]=TID[11:8], bits[3:0]=version(0x3); Byte 1 bits[7:0]=TID[7:0]. (4) Add function signatures: pub fn encode_eoipv6_header(tunnel_id: u16, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<(), EoipError>; pub fn decode_eoipv6_header(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16, usize), EoipError>; pub fn validate_eoipv6_packet(buf: &[u8], ipv6_payload_len: usize) -> Result<(), EoipError>. (5) Add use statement: use crate::error::EoipError. (6) Add worked example in doc comment showing TID=300 (0x12C) encodes to [0x13, 0x2C] and TID=1000 (0x3E8) encodes to [0x33, 0xE8]. (7) Document that TID=0 produces RFC 3378-compliant [0x03, 0x00] packet.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify module compiles with 'cargo check -p eoip-proto'. Write test confirming constants: ETHERIP_VERSION == 0x03, ETHERIP_HEADER_LEN == 2, MAX_EOIPV6_TUNNEL_ID == 4095. Test that module is accessible from lib.rs after adding 'pub mod etherip;' declaration.

subtasks.title: Implement encode_eoipv6_header with tunnel ID validation and nibble-packing
subtasks.description: Implement the encode_eoipv6_header function that validates tunnel_id <= 4095, then writes 2-byte EoIPv6 header with upper 4 bits of tunnel_id packed into high nibble of byte 0, version 0x3 in low nibble of byte 0, and lower 8 bits of tunnel_id in byte 1
subtasks.details: In encode_eoipv6_header function: (1) Validate tunnel_id <= MAX_EOIPV6_TUNNEL_ID (4095), return Err(EoipError::TunnelIdOutOfRange{id: tunnel_id, max: 4095}) if exceeds. (2) Check buf.len() >= 2, return Err(EoipError::PacketTooShort{got: buf.len(), need: 2}) if insufficient. (3) Mask tunnel_id to 12 bits: let tid = tunnel_id & 0x0FFF; (defensive even after validation). (4) Extract high 4 bits: let tid_hi = (tid >> 8) as u8; (5) Extract low 8 bits: let tid_lo = (tid & 0xFF) as u8; (6) Encode byte 0 as: buf[0] = (tid_hi << 4) | ETHERIP_VERSION; combining high nibble of TID with version. (7) Encode byte 1 as: buf[1] = tid_lo; (8) Return Ok(()). (9) Add comprehensive doc comment with worked examples: TID=0 produces [0x03,0x00], TID=300 (0x12C) produces [0x13,0x2C], TID=4095 (0xFFF) produces [0xF3,0xFF]. (10) Add inline comment before buf[0] assignment: // Pack TID[11:8] into high nibble, version 0x3 into low nibble. (11) Document that this encoding makes TID=0 fully RFC 3378 compliant while non-zero TIDs use reserved bits.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: (1) TID=0 produces [0x03,0x00]. (2) TID=300 produces [0x13,0x2C]. (3) TID=1000 produces [0x33,0xE8]. (4) TID=4095 produces [0xF3,0xFF]. (5) TID=4096 returns TunnelIdOutOfRange error. (6) TID=5000 returns TunnelIdOutOfRange error. (7) Buffer with len=1 returns PacketTooShort error. (8) All valid TIDs 0-4095 roundtrip through encode→decode correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement decode_eoipv6_header with version validation and nibble extraction
subtasks.description: Implement the decode_eoipv6_header function that validates buffer length, checks version field equals 0x3, then extracts 12-bit tunnel_id from nibble-packed format by combining high nibble of byte 0 with all of byte 1
subtasks.details: In decode_eoipv6_header function: (1) Check buf.len() >= 2, return Err(EoipError::PacketTooShort{got: buf.len(), need: 2}) if insufficient. (2) Extract version from low nibble of byte 0: let version = buf[0] & 0x0F; (3) Validate version == ETHERIP_VERSION, return Err(EoipError::InvalidVersion{expected: ETHERIP_VERSION, got: version}) if mismatch. This rejects non-0x3 versions from other EtherIP implementations or corrupted packets. (4) Extract high 4 bits of tunnel_id from high nibble of byte 0: let tid_hi = (buf[0] >> 4) as u16; (5) Extract low 8 bits of tunnel_id from byte 1: let tid_lo = buf[1] as u16; (6) Combine to form 12-bit tunnel_id: let tunnel_id = (tid_hi << 8) | tid_lo; (7) Return Ok((tunnel_id, ETHERIP_HEADER_LEN)). The returned header_len allows caller to skip past header to Ethernet payload. (8) Add comprehensive doc comment explaining the reverse operation of encode_eoipv6_header with bit manipulation details. (9) Add worked example showing [0x33,0xE8] decodes to TID=1000: (0x3>>4)<<8 | 0xE8 = 0x03<<8 | 0xE8 = 0x3E8 = 1000. (10) Add inline comment: // Version validation rejects RFC 3378 version 0x2 and future versions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: (1) [0x03,0x00] decodes to TID=0. (2) [0x13,0x2C] decodes to TID=300. (3) [0x33,0xE8] decodes to TID=1000. (4) [0xF3,0xFF] decodes to TID=4095. (5) [0x02,0x00] (version 0x2) returns InvalidVersion error. (6) [0x04,0x00] (version 0x4) returns InvalidVersion error. (7) Buffer with len=1 returns PacketTooShort error. (8) All 4096 valid encoded values 0x03-0xFF3 decode to correct TIDs 0-4095. (9) Property test: forall TID in 0-4095: decode(encode(TID)) == TID.

subtasks.title: Implement validate_eoipv6_packet with length and version checks
subtasks.description: Implement the validate_eoipv6_packet function that performs comprehensive validation: minimum length check for 2-byte header + 14-byte Ethernet frame, version field validation, and IPv6 payload length consistency check
subtasks.details: In validate_eoipv6_packet function with signature (buf: &[u8], ipv6_payload_len: usize) -> Result<(), EoipError>: (1) Check buf.len() >= 16 (2 bytes header + 14 bytes minimum Ethernet frame), return Err(EoipError::PacketTooShort{got: buf.len(), need: 16}) if insufficient. (2) Check buf.len() == ipv6_payload_len for consistency (IPv6 Payload Length field should match actual payload), return Err(EoipError::InvalidEtherIpHeader(format!("buffer length {} does not match IPv6 payload length {}", buf.len(), ipv6_payload_len))) if mismatch. This catches truncated packets. (3) Extract and validate version: let version = buf[0] & 0x0F; if version != ETHERIP_VERSION return Err(EoipError::InvalidVersion{expected: ETHERIP_VERSION, got: version}). (4) Calculate Ethernet frame length: let frame_len = buf.len() - ETHERIP_HEADER_LEN; (5) Validate minimum Ethernet frame: if frame_len < 14 return Err(EoipError::PayloadTooSmall{size: frame_len, min: 14}). (6) Return Ok(()). (7) Add doc comment explaining this is typically called on RX path before demultiplexing to tunnel based on source IP + decoded tunnel_id. (8) Document that this function does NOT decode tunnel_id - caller should use decode_eoipv6_header for that. This separation allows fast-path rejection of invalid packets without tunnel lookup.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: (1) Valid packet [0x03,0x00] + 14-byte Ethernet frame with ipv6_payload_len=16 returns Ok. (2) Valid packet with 60-byte Ethernet frame returns Ok. - decode_udp_shim(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<(u8 inner_type, usize shim_len), EoipError>: Validate magic [0x45,0x4F], type in [0x04,0x06], return type and offset 4

subtasks.title: Create udp_shim.rs module with constants and function signatures
subtasks.description: Create the src/udp_shim.rs file in eoip-proto crate with UDP shim magic bytes constant, inner type constants (0x04 for EoIP, 0x06 for EoIPv6), shim header length definition, and comprehensive documentation about NAT traversal UDP encapsulation format
subtasks.details: Create crates/eoip-proto/src/udp_shim.rs with: (1) Module-level doc comment explaining UDP encapsulation is an EoIP-rs extension (NOT MikroTik-compatible) for NAT traversal, wrapping EoIP/EoIPv6 packets in UDP datagrams with 4-byte shim header [0x45,0x4F,type,0x00]. (2) Define public constants: pub const UDP_SHIM_MAGIC: [u8; 2] = [0x45, 0x4F]; (ASCII "EO"), pub const UDP_SHIM_HEADER_LEN: usize = 4;, pub const UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIP: u8 = 0x04; (matches IP protocol 47 analogy), pub const UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIPV6: u8 = 0x06; (matches IP protocol 97 analogy). (3) Add detailed comment explaining wire format: Byte 0-1: magic [0x45,0x4F], Byte 2: inner_type (0x04=EoIP with 8B header, 0x06=EoIPv6 with 2B header), Byte 3: reserved (0x00, must be zero on TX, ignored on RX). (4) Add function signatures: pub fn encode_udp_shim(inner_type: u8, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<(), EoipError>; pub fn decode_udp_shim(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<(u8, usize), EoipError>; (returns inner_type and shim_len=4). (5) Add use statement: use crate::error::EoipError;. (6) Document default UDP port 26969 in module doc comment (configurable by user). (7) Add wire format diagram showing complete UDP packet structure from protocol.md section 4.2.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify module compiles with 'cargo check -p eoip-proto'. Write test confirming constants: UDP_SHIM_MAGIC == [0x45, 0x4F], UDP_SHIM_HEADER_LEN == 4, UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIP == 0x04, UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIPV6 == 0x06. Test that module is accessible from lib.rs after adding 'pub mod udp_shim;' declaration.

subtasks.title: Implement encode_udp_shim with type validation and fixed header write
subtasks.description: Implement the encode_udp_shim function that validates inner_type is either 0x04 (EoIP) or 0x06 (EoIPv6), checks buffer length, then writes 4-byte shim header with magic bytes, type field, and reserved byte
subtasks.details: In encode_udp_shim function: (1) Validate inner_type is either UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIP (0x04) or UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIPV6 (0x06), return Err(EoipError::InvalidVersion{expected: 0x04 or 0x06, got: inner_type}) for any other value. This prevents accidentally encoding invalid type codes. (2) Check buf.len() >= 4, return Err(EoipError::PacketTooShort{got: buf.len(), need: 4}) if insufficient. (3) Write magic bytes at offset 0-1: buf[0..2].copy_from_slice(&UDP_SHIM_MAGIC);. (4) Write inner_type at offset 2: buf[2] = inner_type;. (5) Write reserved byte at offset 3: buf[3] = 0x00;. (6) Return Ok(()). (7) Add comprehensive doc comment with examples: inner_type=0x04 produces [0x45,0x4F,0x04,0x00] for UDP-wrapped EoIP, inner_type=0x06 produces [0x45,0x4F,0x06,0x00] for UDP-wrapped EoIPv6. (8) Add inline comment before reserved byte: // Reserved byte must be 0x00 on transmission per protocol spec. (9) Document that this function only writes the shim header - caller must write inner EoIP/EoIPv6 header plus Ethernet payload after offset 4.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: (1) encode_udp_shim(0x04, &mut buf) produces [0x45,0x4F,0x04,0x00]. (2) encode_udp_shim(0x06, &mut buf) produces [0x45,0x4F,0x06,0x00]. (3) encode_udp_shim(0x99, &mut buf) returns InvalidVersion error (invalid type). (4) Buffer with len=3 returns PacketTooShort error. (5) Buffer exactly 4 bytes succeeds and writes complete shim. (6) Reserved byte is always 0x00 regardless of input.

subtasks.title: Implement decode_udp_shim with magic validation and type extraction
subtasks.description: Implement the decode_udp_shim function that validates buffer length, checks magic bytes [0x45,0x4F], verifies inner_type is valid (0x04 or 0x06), and returns the inner encapsulation type plus shim header length offset
subtasks.details: In decode_udp_shim function: (1) Check buf.len() >= 4, return Err(EoipError::PacketTooShort{got: buf.len(), need: 4}) if insufficient. (2) Validate magic bytes: if &buf[0..2] != UDP_SHIM_MAGIC, return Err(EoipError::InvalidMagicBytes{expected: UDP_SHIM_MAGIC.to_vec(), got: buf[0..2].to_vec()}). This rejects non-EoIP-rs UDP packets. (3) Extract inner_type from byte 2: let inner_type = buf[2];. (4) Validate inner_type is either 0x04 or 0x06: if inner_type != UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIP && inner_type != UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIPV6, return Err(EoipError::InvalidVersion{expected: format!("0x04 or 0x06"), got: inner_type}). This prevents processing unknown encapsulation formats. (5) Ignore reserved byte at buf[3] per spec (future compatibility). (6) Return Ok((inner_type, UDP_SHIM_HEADER_LEN)). The returned shim_len=4 tells caller where inner packet starts. (7) Add comprehensive doc comment explaining: return value (inner_type, offset_to_inner_packet), caller should then dispatch to eoip_proto::decode_eoip_header or decode_eoipv6_header based on inner_type. (8) Add example: [0x45,0x4F,0x04,0x00,...] returns (0x04, 4) indicating EoIP header starts at offset 4. (9) Add inline comment: // Reserved byte at buf[3] is ignored for forward compatibility with potential future extensions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: (1) decode_udp_shim([0x45,0x4F,0x04,0x00]) returns (0x04, 4). (2) decode_udp_shim([0x45,0x4F,0x06,0x00]) returns (0x06, 4). (3) Wrong magic [0x45,0x4E,0x04,0x00] returns InvalidMagicBytes error. (4) Invalid type [0x45,0x4F,0x99,0x00] returns InvalidVersion error. (5) Buffer with len=3 returns PacketTooShort error. (6) Reserved byte variations [0x45,0x4F,0x04,0xFF] still succeeds (ignored). (7) All valid combinations (magic correct, type 0x04/0x06) decode successfully.

subtasks.title: Implement validation pipeline helper functions in lib.rs
subtasks.description: Update crates/eoip-proto/src/lib.rs with high-level validation pipeline functions that combine decode + validation steps for each protocol mode: validate_and_parse_eoip, validate_and_parse_eoipv6, and validate_and_parse_udp_encap with inner protocol dispatching
subtasks.details: In crates/eoip-proto/src/lib.rs: (1) Import all codec modules: use gre::{decode_eoip_header, validate_eoip_packet}; use etherip::{decode_eoipv6_header, validate_eoipv6_packet}; use udp_shim::{decode_udp_shim, UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIP, UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIPV6};. (2) Implement pub fn validate_and_parse_eoip(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16 tunnel_id, usize payload_offset), EoipError> that: calls validate_eoip_packet(buf, buf.len())?, then decode_eoip_header(buf)?, returns (tunnel_id, 8) for EoIP header length. Document this is for raw IP protocol 47 packets. (3) Implement pub fn validate_and_parse_eoipv6(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16 tunnel_id, usize payload_offset), EoipError> that: calls validate_eoipv6_packet(buf, buf.len())?, then decode_eoipv6_header(buf)?, returns (tunnel_id, 2). Document this is for IPv6 protocol 97 packets. (4) Implement pub fn validate_and_parse_udp_encap(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16 tunnel_id, usize payload_offset), EoipError> that: calls decode_udp_shim(buf)? to get (inner_type, shim_len), then matches on inner_type: if 0x04 calls validate_and_parse_eoip(&buf[4..])? and returns (tunnel_id, 4+8), if 0x06 calls validate_and_parse_eoipv6(&buf[4..])? and returns (tunnel_id, 4+2). Document this handles UDP-encapsulated packets and returns total offset to Ethernet payload. (5) Add module-level doc comment section "# Validation Pipelines" explaining these are convenience functions for RX path fast validation before demux. (6) Re-export udp_shim public types: pub use udp_shim::{UDP_SHIM_MAGIC, UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIP, UDP_INNER_TYPE_EOIPV6, encode_udp_shim, decode_udp_shim};.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests in tests/validation_pipeline.rs: (1) validate_and_parse_eoip with valid 8B header + 60B payload returns correct tunnel_id and offset 8. (2) validate_and_parse_eoipv6 with valid 2B header + 60B payload returns correct tunnel_id and offset 2. (3) validate_and_parse_udp_encap with 4B shim + 8B EoIP + 60B payload returns tunnel_id and offset 12. (4) validate_and_parse_udp_encap with 4B shim + 2B EoIPv6 + 60B payload returns tunnel_id and offset 6. (5) Malformed packets (wrong magic, truncated, invalid types) return appropriate errors. (6) Verify all functions fail fast on first validation error (don't process invalid data).

tasks.title: Build privileged helper binary with TAP creation and FD passing
tasks.description: Implement eoip-helper for root-privileged resource creation, TAP interface setup via ioctl, raw socket creation, SCM_RIGHTS FD passing, and privilege dropping
tasks.details:
Create crates/eoip-helper with Cargo.toml deps: nix={version="0.29", features=["net","ioctl","socket","user"]}, socket2={version="0.5",features=["all"]}, eoip-proto={path="../eoip-proto"}, clap={version="4",features=["derive"]}.

Implement:
- src/tap.rs: create_tap_interface(name: &str) using nix::libc::ioctl with TUNSETIFF, IFF_TAP|IFF_NO_PI flags, return RawFd
- src/rawsock.rs: create_raw_socket_v4() -> socket(AF_INET, SOCK_RAW, 47), create_raw_socket_v6() -> socket(AF_INET6, SOCK_RAW, 97) with proper error handling
- src/fdpass.rs: send_fd_via_scm_rights(unix_sock: RawFd, fd_to_send: RawFd, msg: &HelperMsg) using nix::sys::socket::sendmsg with ControlMessage::ScmRights
- src/privdrop.rs: drop_privileges(uid: u32, gid: u32) calling setgid/setuid after resource creation
- src/main.rs: CLI with clap (#[command] mode: persist|exit, socket_path), main loop listening on Unix socket for DaemonMsg, handling CreateTunnel/Shutdown

Implement MODE_PERSIST (stay alive for dynamic tunnels) vs MODE_EXIT (create all, exit).
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test requiring root: create TAP, verify via `ip link show`, clean up. Mock tests for FD passing protocol. Privilege drop test: verify getuid()==target after drop. Error handling: invalid iface names, socket creation failures
